title: Montenegro-Calla's Medium: an optimized, low-cost culture formulation for axenic growth of Naegleria fowleri
abstract: A serum-free, low-cost culture medium for the axenic growth of Naegleria fowleri, developed as a more accessible and reproducible alternative to commercial formulations for laboratories in low-resource settings.
contributions: A serum-free formulation designed for accessibility and reproducibility
Documented component sourcing aimed at low-resource laboratories
Full formulation and characterization reported in the manuscript under review

links.status: Under review at Journal of Eukaryotic Microbiology; currently in revision

title: Targeting ER stress and mitochondrial pathways in Naegleria fowleri
abstract: A study of endoplasmic reticulum stress and mitochondrial function as potential therapeutic pressure points against Naegleria fowleri, the near-uniformly fatal amoeba behind primary amoebic meningoencephalitis.
contributions: ER stress induced with tunicamycin and thapsigargin, assessed by Western blot (BiP/GRP78)
Mitochondrial function perturbed with metformin, assessed by JC-1 staining
